steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The first sentence (“Why do black-footed cats purr but leopards roar?”) and the concluding sentence (“These traits allow leopards... but not purring”) bookend the explanation. The decisive clue is the word “may,” indicating a hypothesis about the hyoid bone explaining the vocal differences.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: D captures the hypothesis-driven explanation by linking hyoid differences to the purr-vs-roar distinction. A and B focus on details (specific traits in hyoids). C misrepresents the passage’s tone of active hypothesis with “uncertain.” That matches choice D — D is correct.
